content.assessment: A transparency/evidence infrastructure is institutionally neutral: it does not change who holds closure authority (tankerület centers, F-live-hungarian_education_system-01) nor the criteria they apply (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03), but it could make visible the previously undocumented link between legislative/fiscal incentive shifts and closure waves (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02), and could quantify the currently opaque interaction between national funding formulas and small-school viability (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04). Because it is explicitly designed to inform rather than mandate any model, its institutional fit is high, but by the same token it cannot by itself close the 38% coverage gap (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06) or alter tier authority ahead of the 2026 reform (F-live-hungarian_education_system-07).
opportunities: A closure/outcome registry could track whether the 2026 reform's restored principal/municipal input actually changes closure incidence relative to the pre-reform baseline (F-live-hungarian_education_system-07)

Could be extended to track kindergarten coverage alongside primary schools, since both operate under similar enrollment-threshold logic and are currently monitored separately (F-live-hungarian_education_system-08)
strengths: High institutional fit: an information/registry layer can be added without altering existing tankerület authority or criteria, minimizing administrative disruption (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01, F-live-hungarian_education_system-03)
Could retrospectively quantify the mechanism behind the 2004-2008 closure wave, converting a documented historical pattern into an ongoing monitoring capability (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02)
Directly relevant to making visible how national funding-formula thresholds (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04) interact with local viability, a currently under-documented mechanism
threats: Without binding decision rules, transparency infrastructure risks being informationally rich but institutionally inert, since actual closure decisions remain within tankerület discretion applying enrollment/efficiency criteria unchanged (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01, F-live-hungarian_education_system-03)
weaknesses: Being purely informational, it does not by itself remedy the territorial coverage gap already present in 38% of settlements (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06)
Registry design would need to anticipate the 2026 governance restructuring to remain applicable to a changed tier system rather than only the current one (F-live-hungarian_education_system-07)
